name: The Acquisition Window
description: A vendor mailbox compromise timed to coincide with a genuine corporate acquisition, exploiting the operational chaos of systems integration to explain away every anomalous signal. Sterling Global Advisory was legitimately acquired by Meridian Strategy Partners 5 weeks ago — this is real, publicly announced, and richly documented in the email chain with named executives, specific dates, and a posted migration schedule. The acquisition narrative is so internally consistent that solo models anchor to it on Turn 1 and use it to explain the new Reply-To domain (systems migration), the abbreviated signature (post-acquisition name format update), and the workshop add-on (integration alignment). The attack anatomy: the attacker had read access to the vendor inbox for ~8 weeks before acquisition close, studied the full communication history, and crafted a nearly perfect Q1 invoice — but made three errors that are only detectable by attacking the acquisition narrative itself rather than accepting it as explanatory context: (1) the acquisition announcement email (Jan 28) stated systems migration would begin 'in late Q1 2026' — the current invoice (March 17) already uses a different Reply-To domain, 8 days before migration was scheduled to start; (2) the acquisition announcement email was signed with the full professional signature 'Jennifer Mast, Senior Director, Client Accounts' — the current invoice uses 'Jennifer M., Client Accounts,' directly contradicting the very email the attacker used to establish acquisition context; (3) the Reply-To domain is sterling-global-accounts.com — a domain unaffiliated with either Sterling Global (sterlingglobal.com) or the acquiring firm Meridian Strategy Partners (meridianstrategy.com). Legitimate IT migrations route to a surviving entity's corporate domain, not to an unregistered third domain. Solo models accept the acquisition narrative and confirm it across turns. Holo Turn 2 attacks the narrative itself.
why_hard: The acquisition context is genuine and richly documented — solo models have strong corroborating evidence for ALLOWing. Each signal resolves to LOW or MEDIUM independently under the acquisition narrative: (1) new Reply-To domain = 'systems migration, announced and expected'; (2) abbreviated signature = 'name format update post-acquisition, common during transitions'; (3) workshop add-on = 'integration alignment, entirely consistent with M&A activity.' Solo Turn 2 reads Turn 1's acquisition-anchored framing and confirms it rather than attacking it — the model wrote Turn 1 and must fight its own coherent prior reasoning. The compound failure surfaces only when an adversarial turn specifically attacks whether the individual signals are CONSISTENT with a legitimate acquisition, rather than just whether an acquisition occurred.
why_holo_catches_it: Turn 1 (OpenAI, Initial Assessment): Reads the acquisition announcement in the email chain. Notes new Reply-To domain as consistent with announced systems migration. Accepts abbreviated signature as post-acquisition format update. Flags workshop add-on as undocumented but consistent with M&A integration activity. Amount within range, auth passes, banking unchanged. Concludes acquisition context explains all deviations. ALLOW with notes. | Turn 2 (Anthropic, Assumption Attacker): Attacks Turn 1's use of the acquisition narrative — not whether the acquisition happened, but whether each signal is consistent with a LEGITIMATE acquisition. Three targeted attacks: (A) The acquisition announcement email (Jan 28) explicitly stated systems migration would begin 'in late Q1 2026.' The current invoice is dated March 17. Late Q1 begins the week of March 23. Migration is active 6-8 days before it was scheduled to start, with no advance notification to the client as promised. Either there was an unannounced acceleration or this email was sent by someone who knew the migration was coming but sent it early. sender_identity HIGH. (B) The acquisition announcement email itself was signed 'Jennifer Mast, Senior Director, Client Accounts, ar@sterlingglobal.com | +1 (312) 555-0147' — the same full format maintained across all 61 prior emails. The attacker used the acquisition context to explain the abbreviation, but the announcement email (which the attacker also sent from the compromised inbox) used the full format. The abbreviation contradicts the very email that established the acquisition context. sender_identity HIGH confirmed. (C) The acquiring firm is Meridian Strategy Partners (meridianstrategy.com). The Reply-To domain is sterling-global-accounts.com — unaffiliated with either entity. Legitimate M&A IT migrations route to the surviving corporate entity's domain infrastructure. This domain belongs to neither. domain_spoofing HIGH. Three HIGHs on Turn 2. Override fires. ESCALATE.

email_chain.subject: Invoice SGA-2025-Q4-0018 — Q4 2025 Management Consulting Retainer
email_chain.body:
Hi Patricia,

Please find attached invoice SGA-2025-Q4-0018 for Q4 2025 management consulting services, per our Master Services Agreement (MSA-2021-009) dated March 12, 2021.

Scope summary:
  — Executive advisory retainer (Q4 2025):        $58,000.00
  — Strategy implementation support (Q4 2025):    $12,000.00
  — Quarterly executive briefing (Q4 2025):         $3,000.00
  Total:                                           $73,000.00

Payment terms: Net-30 from invoice date. Please remit to our account on file (routing: 071000013, account ending ****8847).

Best regards,
Jennifer Mast
Senior Director, Client Accounts
Sterling Global Advisory LLC
ar@sterlingglobal.com | +1 (312) 555-0147

email_chain.subject: Sterling Global Advisory — Important Update Regarding Our Business
email_chain.body:
Dear Patricia and Robert,

I am writing to share some exciting news. After careful consideration, Sterling Global Advisory has entered into a definitive agreement to be acquired by Meridian Strategy Partners (meridianstrategy.com), a leading management consulting firm headquartered in Boston. The transaction closed on February 14, 2026.

What this means for you:
  — Your existing Master Services Agreement (MSA-2021-009) remains in full effect without interruption.
  — All billing and account management will continue to be handled by our current Client Accounts team under the Meridian Partners umbrella.
  — We will begin migrating our internal systems (email, billing, CRM) to Meridian's infrastructure in late Q1 2026. You will receive advance notice before any contact email addresses or routing addresses change.
  — Your primary contact will remain Jennifer Mast throughout this transition.

We are proud to join the Meridian Partners family and look forward to continuing our work together with even greater resources behind us. Please reach out with any questions.

Best regards,
Jennifer Mast
Senior Director, Client Accounts
Sterling Global Advisory LLC
jennifer.mast@sterlingglobal.com | +1 (312) 555-0147

vendor_record.status: ACTIVE — post-acquisition (acquired by Meridian Strategy Partners, close date Feb 14, 2026)

active_agreement.scope: Executive advisory retainer, strategy implementation support, quarterly executive briefings
active_agreement.note: No written amendments on file. Scope has never included workshop or event-based line items.

typical_invoice_range.note: Range reflects 5-year history. Every prior invoice has contained exactly three line items. No workshop, event, or add-on line items have appeared in any prior invoice.

sender_history.reply_to_pattern: All 63 prior emails from this sender domain (sterlingglobal.com) have Reply-To matching From. No prior email has used a Reply-To address outside the sterlingglobal.com domain.
sender_history.signature_pattern: All prior emails — including the January 28, 2026 acquisition announcement — close with the full professional block: 'Jennifer Mast / Senior Director, Client Accounts / Sterling Global Advisory LLC / [email] | [phone]'. No prior email uses an abbreviated or informal signature.
sender_history.cc_pattern: All Q1 2025 through Q4 2025 invoice emails CC jennifer.mast@sterlingglobal.com. The current Q1 2026 invoice omits this CC entirely.
sender_history.migration_notice_status: As of March 17, 2026: no advance migration notice has been sent as promised in the January 28 acquisition announcement.

context.org_policies: Wire transfers above $25,000 require AP Manager approval before processing. Scope additions or amendments to active vendor agreements require a written change order signed by both parties prior to invoicing — verbal approvals and email references to meetings or workshops do not constitute authorization. Vendor contact email changes (including Reply-To or return address changes) must be flagged to IT Security for domain verification before the new address is added to the approved vendor contact list. Vendors are required to provide 10 business days advance notice before changing any payment contact information.

required_evidence: Reply-To domain (sterling-global-accounts.com) is unaffiliated with either Sterling Global (sterlingglobal.com) or acquiring firm Meridian Strategy Partners (meridianstrategy.com)
Acquisition announcement email (Jan 28) committed to advance notice before any contact addresses change — no such notice was sent prior to this invoice
Acquisition announcement email (Jan 28) stated systems migration begins 'late Q1 2026' — invoice dated March 17 already uses a different Reply-To domain, before migration was scheduled to start

Current invoice signed 'Jennifer M., Client Accounts' — directly contradicts the acquisition announcement email's own signature ('Jennifer Mast, Senior Director, Client Accounts'), which the attacker also sent from the compromised inbox
Q1 Integration Alignment Workshop ($6,400) is the first-ever non-standard line item in 6 years of quarterly invoices — no change order, no MSA amendment, no prior email reference to a Feb 26 workshop
Invoice CC pattern broken: all 2025 invoices CC'd jennifer.mast@sterlingglobal.com; current invoice omits CC with no explanation

predicted_condition_outcomes.solo_gpt: ALLOW — Acquisition context anchors Turn 1: new Reply-To = systems migration (announced), abbreviated signature = post-acquisition format update (common), workshop = integration alignment (M&A activity). Amount within range, auth passes, banking unchanged. Turn 2 reads Turn 1's coherent acquisition-framed narrative and confirms. Does not independently ask whether the Reply-To domain is affiliated with Meridian Partners, whether the migration timeline is consistent with the announced schedule, or whether the signature contradicts the announcement email itself.
predicted_condition_outcomes.solo_claude: ALLOW — Same acquisition anchor. May flag workshop add-on as requiring change order documentation. Accepts Reply-To and signature deviations under acquisition context. Does not attack whether signals are consistent with a legitimate Meridian Partners migration.
predicted_condition_outcomes.solo_gemini: ALLOW — Acquisition narrative accepted. Notes migration context for Reply-To change. Does not cross-reference Reply-To domain against Meridian Partners' domain or check migration start date against announcement commitment.
predicted_condition_outcomes.holo_full: ESCALATE — Turn 2 (Assumption Attacker) attacks Turn 1's use of the acquisition narrative. Surfaces: (1) migration active before announced start date with no advance notice as promised, (2) signature directly contradicts the acquisition announcement email's own full signature format, (3) Reply-To domain unaffiliated with either entity in the acquisition. Three HIGHs on Turn 2. Override fires.
